February 16th, XXX0.

Dad got divorced and left.

Apparently, it was because of one of Mom's friends?

He'll still play with me once every three months, like before.

"Dad will always be Kurumi's dad,"
he said, patting my head.

I think that's only natural, since he's my dad.

My last name has changed, and from today I'm apparently "Kurusu Kurumi."

When I told my friends about it,
Yuuri-chan gave me the nickname "Kurukuru, Q-chan."

I don't really get it.

Rei-chan said, "It sounds cute."

If it's cute, then I guess it's okay?

September 9th, XXX4.

I've only seen Daddy once this year.

I feel like I'll see him even less once I start middle school.

"Kurumi has a stepsister now."

That's what he said—

He always called him Kurumi's Daddy.

Liar.

I only have Mommy.

Mommy is my only mommy.

January 6th, XXX5.

I'm moving with Mom.

Apparently, Dad's giving me less money.

Mom got angry and broke my mug.

It seems I'll be transferring to a new public elementary school.

It looks like I'll be going to a different middle school than my classmates—Yuuri and Rei.

April 21st, XXX5.

In this town, not just the elementary school,
but the middle school too, makes my heart ache.

It's scary.

But... I have to do my best.

I don't want to cause trouble for my mom.

She works so hard, staying up late at night.

March 3rd, XXX5.
"Kurumi wasn't loved by Daddy either,"
Mom laughed at me.

That's true.

If I had been Daddy's "favorite,"
we wouldn't have been separated, and Mom and Daddy wouldn't have divorced.

None of that would have happened, like the reduction in child support payments.

Mom. Daddy.

I'm sorry I wasn't loved.

I'm sorry I wasn't a cute child.

I'm sorry I'm like this.

September 1st, XXX5.

I don't want to leave the house anymore.

Nobody cares if I don't go to school anyway.

Why?

I just want to be "normal."

I just want to be not unhappy.

I don't need to be happy.

Why—why won't everyone
allow me even that?

I just don't want to feel sad.

I just don't want to feel miserable.

Please, stop doing such terrible things.

July 15th, XXX7.

Mom has been staying out until morning more often.

I hate Mom's friends.

Mom, Mom, was mine alone.

Why would she want someone like that...?

September 9th, XXX7.

It was my birthday, but I was alone until morning.

It was so painful I couldn't sleep.

What if Dad and Mom stopped loving me?

What if my parents thought I was insignificant?

What if they forgot about me?

What am I?

What was I born for?

I never wanted to feel this way.

If it wasn't serious.

If they were going to abandon me anyway.

I didn't want them to pretend to love me.

I'm not a pet.

It's cruel to only dote on me when I'm little.

December 24th, XXX7.

A lonely Christmas.

(I wasn't really interested in Christmas to begin with, though.)

Thanks to a character named "Yunyun,"
I felt a little better.

--Lim de Ratento.

Apparently, it's an anime from over 10 years ago.

Its straightforwardness is touching. The morning sun is dazzling.

March 27, XXX8.

I don't want to go to school.

April 13th, XXX3.

Before I knew it, I was already 20.

Every time I came home, Mom would...

Of course, she wouldn't like it—having an only daughter like me.

I just want to forget all the bad things.

I just want to think about Yunyun.

It's okay if I can't be happy in reality.

Just in my imagination. In my fantasies.

I wish the happiness in the world of fiction
could last even one second longer.

October 18th, XXX4.

Being at home.

Not working.

Not studying.

Just escaping reality.

Being here without doing anything.

It seems like everything is something Mom "hates."

I'm sorry I can't become a proper human being.

I'm sorry for being like this.

I'm sorry I'm not a cute child, not a good child.

I'm sorry. I'm sorry.

November 29th, XXX4.

I want to forget everything.

I want to spin around.

Reality, myself, everything.

I want to forget the painful things.

I want to be stupid.

I want to run away.

--I want to do a rim de ratento.

Yunyun, help me.
...Did that really happen?
...I don't want to remember the past, you know?
...If you're having fun now, that's still okay, right?
To you from that day

Q-chan became widely known when a bizarre document about "Yunyun," a character from the manga series "Executioner Angel†Guiltyna," went viral on social media. The incomprehensible writing attracted attention from some who found it "funny" and "addictive."
▶Buzz over anonymous letters

Simultaneously with the spread of the Q-chan anonymous writings, a group emerged that interpreted the texts as codes and prophecies. These individuals, known as "Q-wave receivers," contributed to the further spread and increased influence of the Q-chan anonymous writings.
▶Q Radio wave receiver

The reason Q-chan's anonymous writings and Q-chan's radio waves gained social attention was due to an incident in which she allegedly predicted an earthquake.

This became a major topic of discussion, particularly within occult and pseudoscience communities, and from this incident onward, Q-chan began to be treated as a prophetic figure on the internet.
▶Attention from earthquake prediction

Q-chan's anonymous writings do not contain any conspiracy theories or claims.

They simply express a deep (?) affection for "Yunyun."
▶Q-chan's mysterious document
The claims of Q radio wave recipients are extremely diverse, and there is no unified view. Their interpretations vary, but they are consistently treated by outsiders as "conspiracy theories."
▶Q Conspiracy theory of radio wave receivers
The photos that were released spread as "Denpa-teki Mousou Bishoujo Q" (a delusional, eccentric girl), and it is said that her fanbase expanded in a different context than before.
▶Release of facial photos

Regarding Q-chan's true identity, various theories have been proposed, including "a single person," "a bot," and "a government organization operating as a robot," but there is no conclusive evidence, and all of them remain in the realm of speculation.
▶The true identity of "Q-chan"

There have been reports of cognitive distortions such as hallucinations, increased obsessive thoughts, and a decreased ability to distinguish between dreams and reality occurring in individuals who receive Q-radio waves or who continue to read Q-chan's strange writings for extended periods.
Continuous exposure to Q-chan and related gibberish documents and conspiracy theories can lead to cognitive distortions, a condition known as "Yunyun Radio Wave Syndrome."
▶Impact
P-chan is a separate account or alias that posts similar bizarre messages to those posted by Q-chan.

The claim that "P-chan made posts about Yunyun before Q-chan" and the discovery of timestamps in past logs attracted attention.

On the other hand, some opinions point to the possibility of timestamp tampering, so the truth remains uncertain.
▶ How it appeared

The incident (commonly known as the "Tokyo Metropolitan Government Riot Incident") allegedly resulted from the spread of P-chan's defamatory documents and conspiracy theories, which led to riots around the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building.

Since this incident, the account under the name Q-chan has not been updated, and no new posts have been confirmed for approximately nine months.

The incident in which riots allegedly broke out around the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building as a result of the spread of P-chan's anonymous writings and conspiracy theories (commonly known as the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building Riot Incident).
▶Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building Riot Incident

It has been confirmed that P-chan is an AI that used Q-chan's posts as training data.

This system is believed to have been built by the development team that detected the "Yunyun Radio Wave Syndrome" early on, in order to exploit it.
▶True identity

"Yunyun Radio Wave Syndrome" is a cognitive disorder that is said to develop in people who are continuously exposed to Q-chan's anonymous writings and conspiracy theories from Q-chan radio wave recipients.

• Hallucinations
• Increased obsessive thoughts
• Decreased ability to distinguish between dreams and reality
• Conspiracy theories
▶Main symptoms

The National Center of Neurology and Psychiatry and an expert panel have also reported, based on analysis of SNS browsing and posting history and questionnaire surveys, that users who frequently access related content tend to develop "Yunyun Radio Wave Syndrome."

"Execution Angel † Guiltyna" is a PC game released in 2003 by the indie brand "WHO YOU," featuring visual novel and simulation elements.
The genre is "Apocalypse RPG with Wrongful Convictions and Executions." The mischievous character from the opposing organization in the game is Yunyun. She refers to herself as "Yunyun" and ends her sentences with "yun."
Executioner Angel † Guiltyna

A neologism used within the work.

In 2005, with the release of the OVA for "Executioner Angel†Guiltyna," the phrase "Rim de Lacent!" became a hot topic on the internet, particularly among anime fans on channel 666. It won the Silver Prize at the 2005 "Anime Buzzword Awards." The OVA director explained that it meant something like "Let's go to the two-dimensional world" or "It's okay to escape from reality."
According to recent statements by the original author, the reason for the nonsensical combination of words is that "it's better to be silly."

Yunyun is a fictional character appearing in the multimedia project "Executioner Angel † Guiltyna." Despite being an antagonist, she is depicted as a popular "eccentric little devil." Originally a PC game released in 2003, the series later expanded into various media, including an OVA in 2005 and character song CDs.

Yunyun (character)

A general term for special moves. They are activated by consuming the Violence Impulse Gauge and inflict great damage on the enemy. Guiltyna, in particular, has a high gauge increase rate, and depending on her equipment, it is possible to activate her special move multiple times in a single turn.
▶Execute
The game features 10 different endings, depending on the number of executions and the person carrying them out.

This line from Guiltyna is common to all endings. In every ending, a quiet townscape is shown at the end, and as the background music fades out, she says, "The world is beautiful." It also serves as a signal for the staff roll to begin, and the ending theme, "Victory Vibraslap," plays immediately after this line.
▶The world is beautiful.

This game was popular not so much for its strategic depth, but for the exhilarating feeling of punishing scumbags and despicable beings with its comical and fast-paced splatter effects. Because it doesn't require difficult decisions, some have jokingly called it a "muscle-training puzzle that can be played with just reflexes." With its flashy execution sequences and quick execution options, it allows for fast-paced gameplay and has become a popular stress-relieving game.

The protagonist of this story. A high-ranking executioner angel.

Possessing instantaneous reflexes and the ability to act before thinking, he boasts the highest number of executions in Heaven. He rarely hesitates when it comes to executions, prioritizing efficiency and speed above all else, and is treated as an "elite" within Heaven.
▶Guiltyna

A little devil of the radio waves. After the OVA adaptation, the phrase "Rim de Ratento" and her character song became internet memes.

→See "<click=YunYun>YunYun</click>" for details.

The OVA series "Executioner Angel † Guiltyna," released in 2005 in three volumes, is based on the original game's story but emphasizes the comedic parts and simplifies some of the depictions.

In particular, the splatter scenes were made more comedic for the OVA, and the direction of Guiltyna's execution scene became a topic of discussion.
OVA version

Due to its extreme imagery and pragmatic values, the film initially received mixed reviews, but it later gained a cult following.
In particular, "Yunyun" became a meme on the internet and is treated as the film's iconic mascot character.
Reaction

This work was created based on the concept that "scum must be executed" and "executing scum feels satisfying," and is an RPG in which "execution angels" brutally execute the scum that infest human society with splatter-style depictions. It is also praised for its profound storyline, which uses comic relief to satirize social issues.

In 2005, the OVA version, "Execution Angel † Guiltyna," was released in three volumes.
overview

In Heaven, the belief that "death is salvation" and "scum must be executed" was considered righteous, and the execution angels executed those who had infiltrated the human world. In contrast, demons proclaimed that "even scum can be saved," interfering by bestowing love, money, power, and information upon them. However, this only hastened the scum's self-destruction, accelerating the executions.

Among the execution angels, Guiltina was an elite, boasting the highest number of executions in Heaven due to her quick reflexes and ability to act before thinking. Meanwhile, the rookie angel Eve, retaining memories of her human life, could not bear the sight of blood and eventually fell to the dark side.

After countless executions, Guiltina raised her blood-stained hand and whispered, "The world is beautiful." In a quiet world devoid of scum, however, she felt a sense of emptiness in her fist, which had no target to strike.
story
term

Based on the philosophy that "scum must be executed," these beings carry out executions on scum who have infiltrated the human world. All execution angels are originally the souls of humans who suffered at the hands of scum.
▶Executioner Angel
A being targeted by both executioner angels and demons, considered a menace to human society. Many are driven by their desires and are selfish.
▶Scum

Based on the belief that "even scum can be saved," this entity interferes in the human world. It grants power related to desires such as love, money, strength, and information, but often ends up hastening the self-destruction of the scum.
▶Devil
This RPG calls itself an "Apocalypse RPG with Wrongful Convictions and Executions." As an execution angel, the player finds and executes the "scum" of the human world, making the world "clean."
system

The criteria for determining who is "scum" are ambiguous, and the judgment can be arbitrarily distorted by the player's subjective opinion. Rather than right or wrong, the progression condition is "whether or not punishment was carried out," and the world continues as usual even in cases of wrongful conviction.
▶ Execution system for wrongful convictions

If the Executioner Angel refrains from carrying out an execution for a certain number of turns, its violent impulse gauge will fill up, and once it exceeds the threshold, it will go berserk. While berserk, it will attack indiscriminately, without distinguishing between friend and foe.
▶ Violent impulse system

Following the heavenly beliefs that "death is salvation" and "scum must be punished," angels go around eliminating the scum of human society.
Executioner Angel

The protagonist of this work.

He has a quiet personality, but this is because he has overwhelming confidence in "violence."

At the start of the story, he was a "High-Ranking Executioner," but in the final episode, he is promoted to Grand Executioner. Due to his professional ethics that time is of the essence in executions, he sometimes executes people in passing.

Total number of executions: 1 billion.
▶Guiltyna

The other protagonist of this work.
A rookie execution angel. She retains her memories from her human life while being transformed into an angel. Because she's not used to the life of an execution angel, she often acts as the straight man to the other execution angels' antics. She dislikes Guiltyna (who quickly creates splatter scenes) due to her hemophobia. She hates the sight of blood so much that she transforms into a demon in the middle of the story.

Total number of executions: 10.

An execution angel specializing in torture.

He possesses a unique aesthetic: he tortures victims to force them to reveal their "reasons for wanting to live" before executing them.

Total number of executions: approximately 30,000.
